title: Supported Data Types for DSP Widgets
breadcrumbs: Supported Data Types for DSP Widgets
text: Supported Data Types for DSP Widgets Data in a dynamic server page passes through several layers and transformations as it passes between the database on one end and the client browser on the other. Data formatting and validation may occur either on the client or the server, and this may depend on the Uniface data type, the widget selected, and data entry formatting definitions. The combination of DSP widget and Uniface data type can affect the way the data is displayed, formatted, and validated. Some combinations of widget and data type are not supported, which may result in an exception being displayed in the browser console. For supported widget type and data type combinations, Uniface handles the data type and formatting transformations between the client and the server. However, if you use the JavaScript API to set or get field values, you must ensure that the specified value matches the format defined for the field. The Uniface JavaScript API does not handle data type and formatting conversion. Supported Data types Depending on the type of widget and the HTML input type, the following Uniface data types are supported: S tring B oolean N umeric F loating point D ate T ime D ate T ime I mage For more information, see htmlinput . Unsupported Data Types The following Uniface data types are not supported by DSP widgets: Linear date Linear time Linear datetime Raw Struct XML stream Handle Supported HTML Input Type and Data Type Combinations The following tables show how data formatting is handled for different combinations of widget and data type. ✓ Supported. Best match. ✓ Supported. C Supported. Data is formatted/deformatted on the browser. S Supported. Data is formatted on the server to be compatible with the control type. - Not supported. Data format is incompatible with HTML control type. Table 1.
